---
title: "text_narrative"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L482)

# `text_narrative()`

Create a Text Narrative Block

## Usage

```r
text_narrative(
  text,
  size = 4,
  halign = "left",
  valign = "top",
  padding = 10,
  lineheight = 1.4,
  width = 1,
  wrap_width = NULL,
  ...
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `text` | Character string with marquee-formatted text. Supports markdown: **bold**, *italic*, {#color text. |
| `size` | Text size. Default: 4. |
| `halign` | Horizontal alignment: "left", "center", or "right". Default: "left". |
| `valign` | Vertical alignment: "top", "center", or "bottom". Default: "top". |
| `padding` | Padding around text as a margin() object or numeric vector (top, right, bottom, left). Default: 10 on all sides. |
| `lineheight` | Line height multiplier. Default: 1.4. |
| `width` | Maximum width for text wrapping. Default: 1 (full width). |
| `wrap_width` | Character width to wrap text at. Default: NULL (no wrapping). |
| `...` | Additional arguments passed to `marquee::geom_marquee()`. |

## Value

A ggplot object.

## Description

Creates a ggplot object containing formatted text, suitable for
combining with charts via patchwork. Use for adding story context,
callouts, or explanatory text alongside visualizations.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
library(marquee)
library(patchwork)

text_block <- text_narrative(
    "**Key insight:** Sales in {#E63946 Q4} exceeded
    expectations by 15%, driven primarily by
    the {#2A9D8F new product line}.",
    size = 4
)

my_chart + text_block + plot_layout(widths = c(0.6, 0.4))

## End(Not run)
```
